title: phoradendron staphylinum
intro: Phoradendron staphylinum, also known as Poison Oak, is a species of parasitic plant that belongs to the family Santalaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phoradendron staphylinum include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing inflammation and pain. The herbal preparations of Phoradendron staphylinum typically involve drying and powdering the plant, which can then be made into teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
